intro: Achyronia capitella, also known as Dwarf Thistle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its small, thistle-like flowers and edible parts. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Achyronia capitella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
